description: Thresholds and plain-English language templates for score decomposition. v1.1 reconstructs strings truncated by PDF extraction and adds missing_age key for Tanaka fallback signaling. v1.2 adds disturbance templates for awakenings-per-hour routing. v1.3 adds medium_confidence_full_signals for medium tier when no signals are missing. v1.4 adds hr_elevation signal thresholds and language templates for Load strand HR elevation.

changelog.notes: CRITICAL FIX: Reconstructed all language strings truncated in PDF extraction. Added missing_age confidence language key for Tanaka formula fallback.
Added disturbance language_templates section for awakeningsPerHour explanation routing. Previous implementation incorrectly used sleep_consistency.* keys.
Added confidence_language.medium_confidence_full_signals for medium confidence when all expected signals are present (avoids implying missing data).
Added signal_thresholds.hr_elevation and language_templates.hr_elevation for Load strand workout HR vs resting baseline scoring explanations.
Removed prescriptive language from acwr.very_high. Explanation now describes physiological state only, consistent with design_principles governance rule.
Removed prescriptive sentence from hrv.strong_drop. Description now states physiological condition only.
Added strand_sleep, strand_load, strand_recovery to language_templates for strand-level primaryExplanation narratives. Strand headlines no longer use top signal contributor copy.

design_principles.language_rule: Explanation language describes physiological state only. It does not prescribe action, suggest training decisions, or imply clinical or coaching guidance.
design_principles.rationale: Helix is a readiness instrument, not a coach or clinician. The user interprets and acts. Helix informs.
design_principles.directional_language_permitted: Describing what a signal value means relative to baseline, describing the physiological process associated with a signal, describing a pattern factually without directing a response.
design_principles.enforcement: Agent reviewers (GI, CG) must flag any language string that violates this rule before certification.

hrv.strong_drop: Your nervous system is under notable stress — HRV is well below your baseline.
hrv.significant_drop: Your nervous system is more stressed than usual — HRV is below your normal range. Consider reducing intensity.
hrv.notable_drop: HRV is slightly below your baseline. Your body is processing some accumulated stress.
hrv.within_baseline: HRV is within your normal range.
hrv.notable_rise: HRV is above your baseline — a good sign your nervous system recovered well overnight.
hrv.significant_rise: Strong HRV reading. Your autonomic system is in an excellent recovery state.

resting_hr.strong_rise: Resting heart rate is significantly elevated — a strong signal of fatigue, illness, or incomplete recovery.
resting_hr.significant_rise: Resting heart rate is elevated above your baseline — often a sign of accumulated fatigue or early illness.
resting_hr.notable_rise: Resting HR is slightly above your normal. Worth monitoring over the next day or two.
resting_hr.within_baseline: Resting heart rate is at your normal level.
resting_hr.notable_drop: Resting HR is below your baseline — a positive cardiovascular adaptation signal.

sleep_duration.strong_deficit: Significant sleep deficit last night. Your body had much less restoration time than it needs.
sleep_duration.significant_deficit: You slept less than your usual amount. Recovery processes had a shorter window to complete.
sleep_duration.notable_deficit: Slightly shorter sleep than your normal. Minor impact on today's recovery.
sleep_duration.within_optimal: Sleep duration was within your optimal window.
sleep_duration.surplus: You slept longer than usual — your body may have needed the extra restoration time.

deep_sleep.below_baseline: Deep sleep was below your normal percentage. Physical restoration may be incomplete.
deep_sleep.within_baseline: Deep sleep was within your normal range.
deep_sleep.above_baseline: Strong deep sleep percentage — physical restoration was thorough.

rem_sleep.below_baseline: REM sleep was below your normal percentage. Cognitive restoration and memory consolidation may be affected.
rem_sleep.within_baseline: REM sleep was within your normal range.
rem_sleep.above_baseline: Strong REM percentage — cognitive restoration was thorough.

sleep_consistency.significant_variance: Irregular sleep timing this week is affecting your circadian rhythm. Consistent sleep and wake times improve overall sleep quality.
sleep_consistency.notable_variance: Bedtime has varied more than usual. Consistent timing strongly supports circadian alignment.
sleep_consistency.consistent: Sleep timing has been consistent this week. Your circadian rhythm is well-supported.

disturbance.low: Sleep was undisturbed last night — very few awakenings relative to your normal.
disturbance.within_baseline: Sleep fragmentation was within your normal range.
disturbance.elevated: More awakenings than usual last night. Fragmented sleep reduces restoration quality.

overnight_hr_dip.strong: Your heart rate dropped deeply during sleep — a strong sign your nervous system fully shifted into recovery mode.
overnight_hr_dip.moderate: Moderate overnight heart rate dip. Your nervous system partially recovered during sleep.
overnight_hr_dip.shallow: Shallow overnight heart rate dip suggests your nervous system stayed partially activated during sleep. This can reflect stress, alcohol, or incomplete recovery.

wrist_temperature.elevated: Your wrist temperature was above your normal overnight. This can reflect immune activity, alcohol metabolism, or hormonal variation.
wrist_temperature.stable: Overnight temperature was stable and within your normal range.
wrist_temperature.depressed: Wrist temperature was slightly below your normal overnight. Worth noting but rarely significant in isolation.

acwr.very_high: Training load this week significantly exceeds your recent capacity.
acwr.high: You have trained harder than usual recently. That is a productive stimulus — balance it with adequate recovery.
acwr.optimal: Training load is well-balanced relative to your recent capacity.
acwr.low: Training volume is below your recent baseline. Fine for a recovery week — consider rebuilding gradually if unintentional.

training_volume.high: Training volume this week is elevated relative to your recent output.
training_volume.moderate: Training volume is above your recent average.
training_volume.optimal: Training volume is consistent with your recent baseline.
training_volume.low: Training volume is below your recent baseline.

activity_completion.high: Activity level this week is above your recent norm.
activity_completion.optimal: Activity level is consistent with your recent baseline.
activity_completion.low: Activity level is below your recent baseline.

hr_elevation.high_strain: Average workout heart rate was much higher than your personal resting baseline — a strong cardiovascular strain signal today.
hr_elevation.moderate_strain: Workout heart rate ran noticeably above your resting baseline — meaningful but not extreme strain.
hr_elevation.low_strain: Workout heart rate stayed relatively close to your resting baseline — a lower strain profile for this session block.

respiratory.elevated: Respiratory rate was above your baseline overnight. This can be an early signal of illness, altitude adjustment, or elevated stress.
respiratory.stable: Breathing rate was stable and within your normal overnight range.

strand_sleep.strong: Sleep architecture and duration were both in strong range last night.
strand_sleep.good: Sleep quality was above your normal — most signals were in a positive range.
strand_sleep.moderate: Mixed sleep picture — some signals were strong while others were below your normal.
strand_sleep.low: Sleep quality was below your normal across multiple signals last night.
strand_sleep.poor: Several sleep signals were significantly below your baseline last night.

strand_load.very_high: Training load this week significantly exceeds your recent capacity.
strand_load.high: Training load is above your recent average — a meaningful stimulus.
strand_load.optimal: Training load is well-balanced relative to your recent capacity.
strand_load.low: Training volume is below your recent baseline.
strand_load.very_low: Training volume is significantly below your recent baseline.

strand_recovery.strong: Autonomic recovery looks strong — key signals align with high readiness.
strand_recovery.good: Recovery signals are mostly positive — your nervous system recovered well overnight.
strand_recovery.moderate: Mixed recovery picture — some signals are strong while others are holding the score back.
strand_recovery.low: Recovery signals are below your normal range across multiple markers.
strand_recovery.poor: Autonomic stress indicators are elevated across several signals.

pursue.headline: PURSUE
pursue.subtext: All three strands are in strong alignment. Your body is ready for meaningful effort today.

moderate.headline: MODERATE
moderate.subtext: One strand is recovering. Normal to moderate effort is appropriate — identify the recovering strand and adjust accordingly.

restore.headline: RESTORE
restore.subtext: Your triple helix needs time to recover. Easy movement, good nutrition, and early sleep will rebuild all three strands.

confidence_language.medium_confidence_full_signals: Medium confidence — all expected signals are present; the label reflects policy thresholds rather than missing data.
confidence_language.medium: One signal unavailable — score estimated from remaining data.
confidence_language.low: Multiple signals missing — this is an estimate. Check that Apple Watch was worn during sleep.
confidence_language.missing_hrv: HRV not recorded — autonomic recovery estimated from resting HR and overnight HR dip.
confidence_language.missing_staging: Sleep staging unavailable — architecture estimated from duration and disturbance data.
confidence_language.missing_temperature: Wrist temperature not available on this device — thermal component excluded from sleep score.
confidence_language.missing_age: Date of birth not found in Apple Health — training zones estimated using age 30. Add your date of birth in the Health app for accurate load scoring.
confidence_language.watch_offline: Watch was not worn for part of the night. Score reflects available data only.
